sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Daniel Burton’s transition deal at Health Catalyst (HCAT) details advisory role
feed.summary: Health Catalyst (HCAT) has outlined a transition agreement with former CEO Daniel Burton, who retired on February 12, 2026. He will serve as a strategic advisor until December 31, 2026, receiving a monthly base salary of $10,000. As part of the agreement, Burton will forfeit unvested restricted stock units and provide a general release of claims against the company.

feed.title: Hovnanian Shareholders Approve Expanded Stock Incentive Plan
feed.summary: Hovnanian Enterprises' shareholders approved an expanded stock incentive plan at their 2026 Annual Meeting, increasing the pool of shares for future equity awards by 100,000. They also re-elected directors, ratified the independent auditor, and gave advisory approval for executive compensation. Analysts currently rate Hovnanian (HOV) as a Hold with a $113.00 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, also provides a Neutral assessment.

feed.title: Leonardo DRS EVP and CFO Dippold sells $321k in DRS stock
feed.summary: Michael Dippold, EVP and CFO of Leonardo DRS, sold 7,071 shares of DRS common stock for approximately $320,881 on April 2, 2026. This sale was conducted under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan, even as the stock trades 36% up year-to-date and is considered overvalued by InvestingPro analysis. Dippold also acquired shares through vesting restricted stock units and disposed of others to cover tax withholding requirements, while the company itself has secured a significant military contract and seen analyst price target increases.

feed.title: Circle internet group CAO Schulz sells $104,570 in shares
feed.summary: Circle Internet Group's Chief Accounting Officer, Tamara L. Schulz, sold shares totaling $104,570 on April 2nd under a pre-arranged trading plan, and also disposed of shares worth $98,272 on April 1st to cover tax obligations. Despite these sales, Schulz retains a significant holding in the company. The article also notes analyst ratings for Circle, with Baird and William Blair maintaining "Outperform" ratings, while Morgan Stanley holds an "Equalweight" rating due to regulatory concerns.

feed.title: Rollins Emily, director at Dolby Laboratories, sells $60,080 in stock
feed.summary: Emily Rollins, a director at Dolby Laboratories (NASDAQ:DLB), sold 1,000 shares of Class A Common Stock for a total of $60,080 on April 1, 2026. Following this transaction, Rollins directly owns 12,541 shares. This sale comes after Dolby Laboratories reported strong financial results for Q1 2026, with EPS and revenue surpassing expectations.

feed.title: Leonardo DRS EVP Dorfman sells $211k in stock
feed.summary: Mark Dorfman, EVP of Leonardo DRS (NASDAQ:DRS), sold 4,659 shares for approximately $211,425 on April 2, 2026, under a pre-arranged trading plan. Despite the sale, Dorfman acquired 46,781 shares through restricted stock unit vesting, and the company withheld 23,574 shares for tax obligations. Recent company news includes securing a position on a $25 billion military contract, introducing a new computing system, positive analyst ratings, and a new board appointment.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines (DAL) president receives 2,960 restricted shares in 2026 incentive grant
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines President Peter W. Carter has received a grant of 2,960 shares of restricted common stock as part of the company's 2026 long-term incentive program. This award, approved by the Board’s Personnel & Compensation Committee, brings his direct holdings to 424,704 shares. The transaction is exempt from Section 16(b) under Rule 16b-3(d)(1) as it is a compensation grant rather than an open-market purchase.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) grants 2,470 restricted shares to EVP
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines' EVP & Pres. - International, Alain Bellemare, was granted 2,470 shares of restricted common stock as part of the company's 2026 long-term incentive program. These shares were awarded at no cost and will vest according to an award agreement. Following this transaction, Bellemare directly holds 140,646 shares of Delta common stock.

feed.title: Citigroup Restructures Segment Reporting and Capital Allocation
feed.summary: Citigroup has reorganized its segment reporting for Q1 2026, reclassifying Retail Banking into Wealth and establishing a new U.S. Consumer Cards segment. Additionally, Citi updated its internal tangible common equity (TCE) allocation methodology across its Services, Markets, and Banking segments to better align capital usage with corporate lending economics. These changes do not affect the firm's consolidated results or overall TCE.

feed.title: Does Stryker’s Cyberattack Recovery Reveal Hidden Strengths in Its Long-Term Thesis (SYK)?
feed.summary: Stryker (SYK) experienced a cyberattack in March 2026, which temporarily impacted custom implant operations. However, the company rapidly restored its electronic ordering system and most manufacturing capacity. This quick recovery is prompting investors to evaluate Stryker's resilience and its long-term investment thesis, especially ahead of its Q1 2026 earnings report due on April 30.

feed.title: EPAM Systems Inc stock: AI pivot amid governance debate—what it means for you
feed.summary: EPAM Systems Inc is aggressively shifting its focus to AI and cybersecurity services, aiming to capitalize on growing enterprise demand and drive revenue growth. While the company's financial health is solid with consistent revenue streams and low debt, it faces margin pressures and competition, leading to a discounted valuation compared to its peers. Analysts generally view EPAM positively, citing its strategic pivot as a growth catalyst despite short-term governance concerns, making it an attractive option for North American investors seeking tech exposure with a focus on enterprise AI infrastructure.

feed.title: O’Reilly Auto Parts Opening New Location Near Griffin
feed.summary: O’Reilly Auto Parts is set to open a new location at 4287 US Highway 19 #41 in Griffin, Georgia, adding to its existing 256 stores in the state. The company, which specializes in automotive replacement parts, currently has two other locations in Griffin. An official opening timeline for the new store has not yet been announced.

feed.title: Rockwell Automation (NYSE: ROK) SVP sells shares under 10b5-1 plan
feed.summary: Rockwell Automation's SVP of Software and Control, Bulho Matheus De A G Viera, exercised 882 restricted stock units (RSUs) into common shares and subsequently sold 299 common shares under a pre-arranged 10b5-1 plan. The sale, executed at a weighted average price of $359.00 per share, was primarily to cover tax obligations arising from the RSU vesting on April 1, 2026. Following these transactions, Viera directly holds 2,984 common shares and indirectly holds an additional 5.71 shares through the Company Savings Plan.

feed.title: CVB Financial Corp stock: Merger approvals spark fresh investor interest
feed.summary: CVB Financial Corp (CVBF) has received crucial regulatory approvals for its merger with Heritage Commerce Corp, a move set to expand its presence in California's banking sector. This development comes as the stock trades near $19.50 with an attractive dividend yield, drawing interest from income-focused investors. The merger aims to create cost synergies and broaden market coverage, positioning CVBF as a stronger regional player while maintaining its conservative lending standards.

feed.title: Drugs Made In America II Issues Interim Convertible Note
feed.summary: Drugs Made In America Acquisition II Corp. (DMII) has issued a new $300,000 interim convertible note on March 30, 2026, building upon a previous $150,000 bridge note from March 11, 2026, as part of a $1.4 million financing plan. This new note, maturing in nine months with no interest, will fund expenses for a business combination and offers the investor a 35% discount conversion option into shares of the post-merger entity. The financing signals confidence in DMII's ability to complete an acquisition and provides necessary funds for related professional costs.

feed.title: 3D Systems (NYSE: DDD) CTO uses 4,462 shares for tax withholding
feed.summary: 3D Systems EVP & Chief Technology Officer Charles W. Hull reported a routine tax-related share disposition where 4,462 shares of common stock were withheld to cover tax obligations stemming from the vesting of restricted stock. This transaction, valued at $1.87 per share, was not an open-market sale. Following this, Hull directly holds 190,667 shares and indirectly holds 331,955 shares through a trust.

feed.title: 3D Systems (DDD) EVP sees 8,319 shares withheld to cover RSU tax
feed.summary: Joseph R. Zuiker, Executive Vice President at 3D Systems Corp, had 8,319 shares withheld at $1.87 per share to cover tax obligations related to the vesting of a restricted stock grant. This transaction, which occurred on April 1, 2026, is a routine tax-withholding event and not a market trade. Following this, Zuiker directly holds 283,378 shares of 3D Systems common stock.

feed.title: Director at Dolby Laboratories (DLB) sells 1,000 shares, holds 12,541
feed.summary: Emily Rollins, a director at Dolby Laboratories (DLB), reported an open-market sale of 1,000 shares of Class A common stock at $60.08 per share on April 1, 2026. Following this transaction, she directly holds 12,541 shares, which include 3,908 shares underlying restricted stock units that are subject to forfeiture until they vest. The transaction, filed as a Form 4, indicates a net sale of 1,000 shares valued at $60,080.

feed.title: Kosmos Energy Gains Momentum as Analysts Upgrade Stock Ratings
feed.summary: Kosmos Energy Ltd. (NYSE: KOS) saw its stock price rise by 7.81% due to increasing investor confidence, strategic expansions, and favorable market trends. Analysts, including Johnson Rice, have upgraded their ratings and price targets, indicating optimism for the company's future despite past financial fluctuations. The company's resilience in evolving energy landscapes and recent internal stock purchases by its CFO suggest a promising outlook, though market patience is advised to validate this optimism.

feed.title: 3D Systems (DDD) CEO has 39,951 shares withheld to cover tax obligations
feed.summary: 3D Systems' President and CEO, Jeffrey A. Graves, had 39,951 shares of common stock withheld on April 1st at $1.87 per share to cover tax obligations. This action was related to the vesting of a restricted stock grant from April 1, 2025. After this non-market transaction, Graves directly holds 1,460,241 shares of 3D Systems common stock, signifying a routine tax-withholding event.

feed.title: Capital One’s Dean Lia sells $314k in stock
feed.summary: Dean Lia, President of Banking & Premium Products at Capital One Financial, sold 1,692 shares of company stock for a total of $314,052. This transaction occurred on April 1, 2026, under a pre-arranged trading plan. The article also touches upon recent analyst ratings and the company's executive compensation plans, including CEO Richard D. Fairbank's $40 million pay package for 2025.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) director converts 14,500 RSUs into common stock
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise director Raymond J. Lane converted 14,500 restricted stock units (RSUs) into common stock, increasing his direct holdings to 989,564 shares. The RSUs, originally granted in May 2025, vested on the date of the company's 2026 Annual Stockholders Meeting and included dividend equivalent rights. This transaction is an exercise and conversion of equity awards, not an open-market purchase or sale.

feed.title: Leonardo DRS (DRS) CEO reports RSU vesting, awards and tax-share withholding
feed.summary: Leonardo DRS President and CEO John Baylouny reported significant equity compensation transactions on April 1, 2026. These included the exercise or conversion of 26,960 performance and time-based restricted stock units into common stock and additional stock awards. To cover tax obligations, 42,016 shares were withheld by the company at prices around $45.85, a tax-withholding mechanism rather than a market sale.

feed.title: Raymond James Financial stock: Steady performer with 9% upside potential?
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial is presented as a stable investment opportunity in the wealth management sector, particularly for North American investors. The article highlights its diversified business model, client-focused services, and strong financial health as key strengths. Analysts foresee a 9% upside potential from current levels, recommending it for buy-and-hold investors despite some market risks.

feed.title: 3D Systems (DDD) SVP has 3,863 shares withheld for tax obligations
feed.summary: 3D Systems Corp (DDD) Senior Vice President, General Counsel, and Secretary Andrew William Banasick Wright had 3,863 shares of common stock withheld at $1.87 per share to cover tax obligations following the vesting of a restricted stock grant. This transaction was a routine tax-withholding disposition and not an open-market sale. After this event, Wright directly owns 142,781 shares of 3D Systems common stock.

feed.title: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN): Billionaire Steve Cohen Is Buying This Tech Stock
feed.summary: Billionaire Steve Cohen's Point72 Asset Management has significantly increased its stake in Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN), now holding 5.2 million shares. This move highlights Amazon's strong performance, particularly its Amazon Web Services (AWS) division, which is experiencing a growth resurgence driven by AI and proprietary chips. The article suggests that while Amazon is a strong investment, other AI stocks might offer even higher, quicker returns.

feed.title: Microsoft Corporation (MSFT): Billionaire Steve Cohen Trims Position
feed.summary: Billionaire Steve Cohen's fund has trimmed its position in Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ:MSFT) by 20%, now holding 2 million shares. Despite this reduction, the fund has maintained a long-term bullish view on Microsoft, seeing potential in its Microsoft 365 Copilot transition to Agentic AI and its move to control hardware costs with custom Maia AI chips. The article also mentions that elite hedge funds favor MSFT and points to other AI stocks with potentially greater upside and less risk.

feed.title: [Form 4] Circle Internet Group, Inc. Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Circle Internet Group, Inc.'s CFO, Jeremy Fox-Geen, engaged in a series of transactions including exercising stock options, selling Class A common stock, and having shares withheld for tax obligations. These activities, partially conducted under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan, resulted in a net sale of 11,438 shares and left him with 363,726 shares (outright and issuable upon RSU vesting) and 1,152,806 stock options. The filing indicates a negative sentiment and a very high filing impact for CRCL.

feed.title: [Form 4] Circle Internet Group, Inc. Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Nikhil Chandhok, Chief Product & Tech. Officer for Circle Internet Group, Inc. (CRCL), reported a routine tax-related disposition of 3,815 shares of Class A common stock on April 1, 2026. The shares, valued at $95.41 each, were withheld to cover tax obligations upon the vesting of restricted stock units. Following this transaction, Chandhok beneficially owns 536,561 shares of Class A common stock.

feed.title: Circle Internet Group (CRCL) CEO exercises stock units, uses shares to cover taxes
feed.summary: Circle Internet Group (CRCL) Chairman and CEO Jeremy Allaire exercised 30,388 restricted stock units and other derivative securities on April 1, 2026, acquiring Class A common stock. Concurrently, 8,404 shares of Class B common stock were used to cover tax obligations, a tax-withholding disposition rather than an open-market sale. Following these transactions, Allaire directly holds 294,201 shares of Class A common stock and 15,866,559 shares of Class B common stock, with additional indirect holdings through various trusts.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) director defers 14.5K RSUs into stock
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) director Jean M. Hobby exercised 14,499.0518 restricted stock units (RSUs) into common shares on April 1, 2026, and elected to defer receiving the common stock until her board service ends. Following this transaction, she indirectly holds 47,490.5734 shares and directly holds 32,279 shares, which includes vested RSU dividend equivalent rights. The RSUs, originally granted in May 2025, cliff vested at the 2026 Annual Stockholders Meeting.

feed.title: Circle Internet Group (CRCL) director sells 35,000 shares under 10b5-1 plans
feed.summary: Circle Internet Group director Neville Patrick Sean reported the sale of 35,000 shares of Class A common stock, valued at $98.04 per share, totaling approximately $3.43 million. These transactions, which involved exercising options, converting Class B to Class A shares, and selling under Rule 10b5-1 trading plans, were dated April 1, 2026. Following these sales, Sean retains substantial holdings in both Class A and Class B common stock, directly and indirectly through trusts.

feed.title: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA): Billionaire Steve Cohen Has a Long-Term Bet on This Stock
feed.summary: Billionaire Steve Cohen's Point72 Asset Management has significantly increased its long-term bet on NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA), holding nearly 10 million shares by the end of Q4 2025. This move highlights hedge fund confidence in NVIDIA's role in the AI supercycle, especially as the company projects massive sales for its Blackwell and upcoming Vera Rubin chip systems. The article notes a shift in focus towards AI inference and critical efficiency gains as reasons for this investment, while also suggesting other AI stocks might offer greater upside.

feed.title: Capital One (COF) banking president sells 1,692 shares via 10b5-1 plan
feed.summary: Dean Lia, President of Banking & Premium Products at Capital One Financial (COF), sold 1,692 shares of common stock at $185.61 per share through a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established on August 14, 2025. Following this transaction, Lia continues to hold 65,454 shares of Capital One common stock. This sale, valued at $314,052, was reported in an SEC Form 4 filing, indicating it was scheduled in advance rather than being discretionarily timed.

feed.title: [Form 4] CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORP Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Celia Karam, President of the Retail Bank at CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORP (COF), reported an open-market sale of 1,099 shares of common stock on April 1, 2026, at an average price of $185.61 per share. This transaction was executed under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established on August 14, 2025. Following the sale, Karam still holds 63,323 shares, indicating that this was a small, routine disposition rather than a significant change in her equity stake or a strong directional signal.

feed.title: Datadog (DDOG) grants 28,625 RSUs to general counsel Kerry Acocella
feed.summary: Datadog, Inc. (DDOG) has granted 28,625 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) of Class A Common Stock to its General Counsel and Secretary, Kerry Acocella. The grant, with a purchase price of $0.00 per share, increases Acocella's direct holdings to 154,346 shares. The RSUs will vest in installments starting March 1, 2027, contingent on her continuous service with the company.

feed.title: Datadog (DDOG) Chief People Officer granted 25,354 RSUs
feed.summary: Datadog's Chief People Officer, David Galloreese, received a grant of 25,354 restricted stock units (RSUs) of Class A common stock at no cash cost. This grant, detailed in a Form 4 SEC filing, increases his direct holdings to 130,886 shares following the award. The RSUs will vest over time, with the first portion on March 1, 2027, and the remainder in quarterly installments, contingent on his continuous service.

feed.title: Datadog (DDOG) director sells 20,000 shares in Rule 10b5-1 trade
feed.summary: Datadog director Amit Agarwal reported the sale of 20,000 Class A Common Stock shares on April 1, 2026, at $120.53 per share, totaling $2.41 million. This transaction stemmed from a conversion of Class B shares and was executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established on December 8, 2025. Following the sale, his family trust retains significant indirect holdings in both Class A and Class B shares.

feed.title: Datadog (DDOG) CEO receives 130,858 RSUs vesting from 2027
feed.summary: Datadog, Inc.'s CEO, Olivier Pomel, has been granted 130,858 restricted stock units (RSUs) of Class A Common Stock. This compensation award will bring his directly held Class A Common Stock position to 835,679 shares. The RSUs will begin vesting on March 1, 2027, with 12/48 of the shares vesting then, followed by 12 equal quarterly installments, contingent on his continuous service with the company.

feed.title: Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) awards 54,797 RSUs to Chief Revenue Officer
feed.summary: Datadog's Chief Revenue Officer, Sean Michael Walters, was granted 54,797 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) as a compensation award, not an open-market purchase. These RSUs vest over time, with the first portion vesting on March 1, 2027, and subsequent installments quarterly, contingent on his continued service. Following this grant, Walters directly holds 293,181 shares of Class A Common Stock.

feed.title: Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) CTO receives 94,054 RSUs, holds 531,480 shares
feed.summary: Datadog's Chief Technology Officer, Alexis Le-Quoc, was granted 94,054 restricted stock units (RSUs) as part of his compensation, not an open-market purchase. These RSUs will vest over time, starting March 1, 2027, contingent on his continuous service with the company. Following this grant, Le-Quoc directly holds 531,311 Class A Common Stock shares, with an additional 169 shares held indirectly through a revocable trust.

feed.title: Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) director gets equity grant in lieu of cash
feed.summary: Datadog director Dominic Phillips received a grant of 40 shares of Class A Common Stock, valued at $118.05 per share, as compensation in lieu of cash fees. This transaction, detailed in a Form 4 filing, increases his direct holdings to 5,191 Class A shares. The equity grant was issued under the company’s non-employee director compensation policy.

feed.title: [Form 4] Datadog, Inc. Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Datadog, Inc.'s Chief Financial Officer, David M. Obstler, reported an acquisition of 59,295 shares of Class A Common Stock through restricted stock units (RSUs). These RSUs vest over time, starting March 1, 2027, and are contingent on continuous service. Following this grant, Obstler holds 525,914 shares directly.

feed.title: Datadog (DDOG) COO Adam Blitzer receives 59,295 RSU stock award
feed.summary: Datadog, Inc. COO Adam Blitzer has been awarded 59,295 shares of Class A Common Stock in restricted stock units (RSUs). These RSUs carry no purchase price and will increase his direct holdings to 289,615 shares once fully vested. The vesting schedule begins on March 1, 2027, with subsequent installments quarterly, provided Blitzer remains in continuous service with the company.

feed.title: Datadog (DDOG) Chief Product Officer receives 51,116-share RSU grant
feed.summary: Datadog, Inc.'s Chief Product Officer, Li Yanbing, was granted 51,116 restricted stock units (RSUs) of Class A Common Stock. These RSUs will vest over time, with the first portion vesting on March 1, 2027, provided Li remains in continuous service with the company. Following this grant, Li Yanbing directly holds 305,117 shares of Class A Common Stock.

feed.title: TotalEnergies (NYSE: CWEN) updates Clearway stake and plans voting trust
feed.summary: TotalEnergies has filed an amended Schedule 13D for Clearway Energy, Inc. (CWEN), significantly updating its ownership and governance. The filing indicates TotalEnergies' beneficial ownership in Class A Common Stock is now 0.1%, effectively ceasing its status as a greater-than-five-percent beneficial owner of Class A shares. However, it still holds a substantial 49.3% beneficial ownership of Class C Common Stock and plans for a voting trust to maintain its relative voting power during a proposed Class A to Class C conversion.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty (NASDAQ: ULTA) seeks votes on directors, officer exculpation, forum clause
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty is seeking shareholder votes on several key matters at its virtual annual meeting on June 9, 2026. These proposals include the election of ten directors, approval of Certificate of Incorporation amendments for officer exculpation and a forum selection clause, ratification of Ernst & Young LLP as auditor, approval of executive compensation, and the 2026 Incentive Award Plan. The proxy statement details the company's governance practices, director compensation, executive compensation structure, and a new severance plan, highlighting a focus on aligning management and shareholder interests.

feed.title: FirstEnergy (FE) director receives 837 phantom stock units as deferred board pay
feed.summary: FirstEnergy (FE) director Melvin D. Williams was granted 837 phantom stock units as deferred compensation on April 1, 2026, under the company's 2020 Incentive Compensation Plan. These units, economically equivalent to one common share each, are payable in cash or shares after his board service concludes. Following this grant, Williams holds 18,290.4465 phantom stock units and 2,892.415 direct common stock shares, reflecting routine equity-based board compensation rather than an open-market transaction.

feed.title: Smucker (SJM) director Tarang Amin receives 262 Deferred Stock Units award
feed.summary: J. M. Smucker Co. (SJM) director Tarang Amin was granted 261.808 Deferred Stock Units (DSUs), increasing his total deferred holdings to 6,958.414 units. These DSUs are a form of director compensation that will convert into common shares on a 1-for-1 basis after his service as a director concludes. The reported amount also includes shares accumulated through the company's dividend reinvestment plan.

feed.title: J M Smucker (SJM) director Perry Kirk awarded deferred stock units
feed.summary: J. M. Smucker Co. director Perry Kirk was awarded 261.808 Deferred Stock Units (DSUs) on Common Shares, increasing his directly held DSUs to 22,527.129. These DSUs, granted at a $0.00 price, are payable 1-for-1 in Common Shares after his directorship concludes and include shares from the company's dividend reinvestment plan. This transaction, reported via a Form 4 SEC filing, has a neutral impact and sentiment according to Rhea-AI.
